tasks.title: Create chat domain models
tasks.description: Create the core domain model file for the chat feature: message types, sender types, chat types, and conversation summary.
acceptance_criteria: ChatType enum exists with Human and Ai values
SenderType enum exists with User, Coach, Ai values
MessageContent is a sealed class with Text(text: String) and Image(url: String, thumbnailUrl: String?) subclasses
ChatMessage data class exists with: id, userId, chatType, senderType, content, createdAt (Instant), readAt (Instant?)
ChatConversationSummary data class exists with: chatType, lastMessage (ChatMessage?), unreadCount (Int)
File compiles without errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow the pattern of existing domain models (e.g. Nutrition.kt, Workout.kt). Use kotlinx.datetime.Instant for timestamps. No framework imports in domain layer.

tasks.title: Create ChatMessageDto and mapper
tasks.description: Create the Supabase DTO for chat messages and a mapper to/from the domain model.
acceptance_criteria: ChatMessageDto is @Serializable with fields: id, user_id, chat_type, sender_type, content_type, text_content (nullable), image_url (nullable), created_at, read_at (nullable)
Extension function ChatMessageDto.toDomain(): ChatMessage exists in the same file
Mapper correctly maps chat_type string ('human'/'ai') to ChatType enum
Mapper correctly maps sender_type string to SenderType enum
Mapper reconstructs MessageContent from content_type + text_content/image_url fields
File compiles without errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow existing DTO patterns (e.g. WorkoutDto.kt, NutritionDto.kt). Use @SerialName for snake_case fields. Parse Instant from ISO string using Instant.parse().

tasks.title: Add Supabase schema migration and BuildKonfig fields
tasks.description: Create the chat_messages Supabase SQL schema and add AI-related BuildKonfig fields to the build config.
acceptance_criteria: SQL file creates chat_messages table with columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (UUID FK auth.users), chat_type (TEXT CHECK human/ai), sender_type (TEXT CHECK user/coach/ai), content_type (TEXT CHECK text/image), text_content (TEXT nullable), image_url (TEXT nullable), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()), read_at (TIMESTAMPTZ nullable)
SQL includes index on (user_id, chat_type, created_at DESC)
SQL enables RLS with policy: users see only their own messages
build.gradle.kts buildkonfig block adds: AI_COACH_ENABLED (Boolean, default false), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (String, default empty), AI_COACH_SYSTEM_PROMPT (String, default fitness coach prompt)
local.properties keys documented in a comment in build.gradle.kts
tasks.implementation_notes: Check existing buildkonfig block for STRING type import — add Boolean type import from buildkonfig. Default AI_COACH_ENABLED to false so it is off unless explicitly enabled. Use a sensible default system prompt string directly in defaultConfigs.

tasks.title: Create ChatRepository interface and ChatAiProvider interface
tasks.description: Define the contracts for the chat repository and the AI provider abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: ChatRepository interface has: observeMessages(userId, chatType): Flow<List<ChatMessage>>, sendMessage(userId, chatType, content): Result<ChatMessage>, markMessagesRead(userId, chatType): Result<Unit>, getConversationSummaries(userId): Result<List<ChatConversationSummary>>
ChatAiProvider interface has: streamResponse(systemPrompt, history, userMessage): Flow<String>
Both interfaces are in the correct packages (domain/repository and data/ai respectively)
No framework or Supabase imports in ChatRepository interface
Files compile without errors
tasks.implementation_notes: ChatAiProvider lives in data/ai (not domain) as it is an infrastructure concern, not business logic. ChatRepository follows the same pattern as WorkoutRepository and MealRepository interfaces.

tasks.title: Implement ChatRemoteDataSource
tasks.description: Implement the Supabase data source for chat: fetch message history via Postgrest and subscribe to new messages via Realtime.
acceptance_criteria: fetchMessages(userId, chatType, limit, offset) queries chat_messages table filtered by user_id and chat_type, ordered by created_at DESC, returns List<ChatMessageDto>
sendMessage(dto) inserts a new row into chat_messages, returns the inserted ChatMessageDto
observeNewMessages(userId, chatType) subscribes to Supabase Realtime on chat_messages table filtered by user_id + chat_type and emits ChatMessageDto via Flow
markRead(userId, chatType) updates read_at = NOW() for all null read_at rows matching user_id + chat_type
Constructor takes SupabaseClient (injected)
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow WorkoutRemoteDataSource and MealRemoteDataSource patterns for Postgrest queries. For Realtime, follow the Supabase KMP Realtime docs pattern — use supabase.realtime.createChannel() and filter by eq('user_id', userId). Realtime subscription should be started lazily and cancelled when the Flow collector cancels.

tasks.title: Implement Claude AI provider and Gemini stub
tasks.description: Implement ClaudeAiProvider using Ktor to call the Anthropic REST API with SSE streaming, and a GeminiAiProvider stub.
acceptance_criteria: ClaudeAiProvider implements ChatAiProvider
ClaudeAiProvider sends POST to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with model claude-sonnet-4-6, stream=true, Authorization header with api key, anthropic-version header
Request body maps history List<ChatMessage> to Anthropic messages format (role: user/assistant, content: string)
Response is parsed as SSE stream; text delta chunks are emitted via Flow<String>
GeminiAiProvider implements ChatAiProvider and throws NotImplementedError('Gemini provider not yet implemented')
Both classes take HttpClient and apiKey as constructor parameters
Files compile without errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Use Ktor's HttpStatement and execute { response -> response.bodyAsChannel() } to stream the SSE response. Parse lines that start with 'data: ' and extract the text delta from the JSON payload. Map SenderType.User to role 'user' and SenderType.Ai to role 'assistant' when building history.

tasks.title: Implement ChatRepositoryImpl
tasks.description: Implement the ChatRepository interface, combining ChatRemoteDataSource for human chat and ChatAiProvider for AI responses.
acceptance_criteria: ChatRepositoryImpl implements ChatRepository
observeMessages() merges initial Postgrest fetch with Realtime new-message events into a single Flow<List<ChatMessage>>, sorted by createdAt ASC
sendMessage() for ChatType.Human inserts via ChatRemoteDataSource and maps result to domain
sendMessage() for ChatType.Ai inserts the user message first, then returns Result.success with the pending message (AI response is handled separately by SendAiChatMessageUseCase)
getConversationSummaries() fetches the latest message per chat_type and counts unread messages
All Results wrap exceptions correctly using runCatching
Constructor takes ChatRemoteDataSource and ChatAiProvider
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow the pattern of MealRepositoryImpl and WorkoutRepositoryImpl. For observeMessages(), use a MutableStateFlow<List<ChatMessage>> seeded by the initial fetch, then merge with realtime events using Flow operators. Mark existing messages as read via markMessagesRead when observing starts.

tasks.title: Implement chat use cases
tasks.description: Create the four use cases that encapsulate chat business logic.
acceptance_criteria: ObserveChatMessagesUseCase(repo).invoke(userId, chatType): Flow<List<ChatMessage>> delegates to ChatRepository.observeMessages()
SendHumanChatMessageUseCase(repo).invoke(userId, content): Result<ChatMessage> delegates to ChatRepository.sendMessage() with ChatType.Human
SendAiChatMessageUseCase(repo, provider).invoke(userId, history, userMessage): Flow<String> calls ChatAiProvider.streamResponse() with system prompt from ChatConfig
MarkMessagesReadUseCase(repo).invoke(userId, chatType): Result<Unit> delegates to ChatRepository.markMessagesRead()
All use cases follow the existing operator-fun invoke pattern from other use cases in the project
Files compile without errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Read GetAssignedWorkoutsUseCase.kt and LogMealUseCase.kt for the invoke-pattern. SendAiChatMessageUseCase also inserts the user's message into the repo before streaming the AI response, so history is persisted. Fetch ChatConfig.aiSystemPrompt inside the use case.

tasks.title: Register chat components in DI module
tasks.description: Add the chatModule Koin module and wire up all chat dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: chatModule is defined as a Koin module containing: ChatRemoteDataSource (single), ClaudeAiProvider as ChatAiProvider (single), ChatRepositoryImpl as ChatRepository (single), all 4 use cases (factory), ChatViewModel (viewModel with userId and chatType params)
chatModule is added to the appModules list
ClaudeAiProvider receives HttpClient (get()) and BuildKonfig.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Project compiles without errors after adding the module
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow the existing module structure in AppModule.kt — add chatModule as a new val and append it to appModules. Use single<ChatRepository> { ChatRepositoryImpl(get(), get()) } and single<ChatAiProvider> { ClaudeAiProvider(get(), BuildKonfig.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) }.

tasks.title: Create ChatState and ChatIntent
tasks.description: Define the UI state and intent sealed interface for the chat screen.
acceptance_criteria: ChatState is a data class with: messages (List<ChatMessage>, default empty), isLoading (Boolean), isSending (Boolean), isAiStreaming (Boolean), streamingText (String, default empty), error (String?, default null), hasMorePages (Boolean)
ChatIntent is a sealed interface with: LoadMessages, LoadMoreMessages, SendTextMessage(text: String), SendImageMessage(imageBytes: ByteArray), MarkAllRead
Both files follow the exact same pattern as HomeState/HomeIntent and WorkoutState/WorkoutIntent
Files compile without errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Read HomeState.kt and HomeIntent.kt for the exact pattern to follow. ChatIntent uses sealed interface (not sealed class) to match existing patterns.

tasks.title: Implement ChatViewModel
tasks.description: Implement the ViewModel for the chat screen, handling message loading, sending, AI streaming, and read state.
acceptance_criteria: ChatViewModel takes: ObserveChatMessagesUseCase, SendHumanChatMessageUseCase, SendAiChatMessageUseCase, MarkMessagesReadUseCase, userId: String, chatType: ChatType
init block dispatches LoadMessages intent
LoadMessages starts collecting ObserveChatMessagesUseCase flow, updates state.messages, calls MarkAllRead on first load
SendTextMessage for Human chat calls SendHumanChatMessageUseCase, sets isSending=true, clears error on success
SendTextMessage for Ai chat calls SendAiChatMessageUseCase, sets isAiStreaming=true, accumulates chunks in streamingText, appends final message to messages list when stream ends
Error cases set state.error with exception message
Follows the onIntent(intent) dispatch pattern from HomeViewModel
tasks.implementation_notes: Read HomeViewModel.kt for the exact ViewModel pattern. For AI streaming: collect Flow<String> chunks in a coroutine, update streamingText on each chunk, and when complete add the full message to the messages list and clear streamingText. Use viewModelScope.launch for all coroutines.

tasks.title: Build chat UI components
tasks.description: Create the reusable Compose components for the chat screen: message bubbles, input bar, and image preview.
acceptance_criteria: ChatBubble(message: ChatMessage, isOwn: Boolean) renders a message bubble aligned right for own messages, left for others, using MaterialTheme colors
ChatBubble shows text content for MessageContent.Text and a Coil AsyncImage for MessageContent.Image
ChatBubble shows timestamp in a small label below the bubble
ChatInputBar(text, onTextChange, onSend, onImageAttach, isSending) renders a TextField with send button and image attach icon button
ChatInputBar disables send button when text is blank or isSending=true
ImageMessagePreview(imageBytes, onRemove) renders a thumbnail of a selected image with a remove button
All components use existing CoachFoskaTheme colors and typography
tasks.implementation_notes: Use Coil AsyncImage (already a dependency) for image display. For bubble alignment use Row with Arrangement.End/Start. Check CoachComponents.kt for existing component style reference. The image attach button will trigger the MediaCapture flow (already exists) — just wire the onClick for now.

tasks.title: Build ChatScreen composable
tasks.description: Build the main chat screen that renders the message thread and input bar, shared by both human coach and AI coach modes.
acceptance_criteria: ChatRoute(userId, chatType, onBackClick) composable exists as the nav entry point
Collects ChatViewModel state via collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
Shows a LazyColumn of ChatBubble items, newest at bottom (reverseLayout or scroll to bottom on new message)
Shows streaming AI response as a partial bubble at the bottom when isAiStreaming=true and streamingText is not empty
Shows LinearProgressIndicator when isLoading=true
Shows error Snackbar when state.error is not null
ChatInputBar is shown at the bottom; SendTextMessage intent dispatched on send
Screen title in TopBar: 'COACH' for Human, 'AI COACH' for Ai
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow the structure of MealPlanScreen.kt and WorkoutListScreen.kt for the Route/Screen composable split pattern. Use rememberLazyListState() and LaunchedEffect to auto-scroll to bottom when messages.size changes. AI streaming bubble can reuse ChatBubble with a placeholder ChatMessage.

tasks.title: Build ChatHubScreen
tasks.description: Build the hub screen that shows human coach and AI coach conversation entry points.
acceptance_criteria: ChatHubRoute(userId, onHumanCoachClick, onAiCoachClick) composable exists
Always shows a conversation row for Human Coach with name, last message preview, and unread count badge
Shows AI Coach row only when ChatConfig.aiCoachEnabled is true
Each row shows avatar placeholder, coach name, last message snippet (max 1 line), and timestamp
Tapping a row calls the corresponding onXxxClick callback
Loading state shows placeholder shimmer or CircularProgressIndicator
tasks.implementation_notes: ChatHubScreen needs its own lightweight ViewModel or can read conversation summaries from the existing ChatViewModel. Prefer a separate lightweight HubViewModel that calls GetConversationSummariesUseCase (add it to ChatRepository). Keep this screen simple — it is a list of 1-2 items. Check ChatConfig.aiCoachEnabled using BuildKonfig.AI_COACH_ENABLED directly.

tasks.title: Wire up navigation and bottom nav
tasks.description: Add Chat to the bottom navigation, define the chat routes in the nav graph, and update App.kt.
acceptance_criteria: Routes.kt adds: @Serializable object Chat, @Serializable object HumanCoachChat, @Serializable object AiCoachChat
BottomNavBar adds Chat tab between Workout and Nutrition (order: Home, Workout, Chat, Nutrition, Profile)
BottomNavBar Chat tab icon uses Icons.Default.Chat (or AutoMirrored Chat icon)
BottomNavBar accepts optional unreadCount: Int and renders BadgedBox on Chat tab when unreadCount > 0
App.kt adds Chat to bottomTabRoutes and authRoutes exclusion
App.kt adds composable<Chat> routing to ChatHubRoute
App.kt adds composable<HumanCoachChat> and composable<AiCoachChat> routing to ChatRoute with appropriate chatType
App.kt selectedTab logic includes Chat detection
App.kt topBarTitle includes 'CHAT' for HumanCoachChat and AiCoachChat destinations
Project compiles and nav works end-to-end
tasks.implementation_notes: Read App.kt carefully before modifying — the bottomTabRoutes list, selectedTab derivedStateOf, and topBarTitle derivedStateOf all need Chat entries. For the BadgedBox, the unreadCount will come from a future integration point; wire it up as 0 for now. Use Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.Chat if available, otherwise Icons.Default.MailOutline as fallback.

tasks.title: Add coach message preview card to Home screen
tasks.description: Update HomeState and HomeViewModel to load the last coach message, and add a CoachMessagePreviewCard to the Home screen.
acceptance_criteria: HomeState gains field: lastCoachMessage: ChatMessage? = null
HomeViewModel loads lastCoachMessage concurrently with existing profile/workout/nutrition data using async/await
HomeViewModel uses ObserveChatMessagesUseCase to get first message from Human coach thread
HomeScreen shows CoachMessagePreviewCard at the top of the content when lastCoachMessage != null
CoachMessagePreviewCard shows coach avatar placeholder, message preview text (max 2 lines), and a 'Reply' button
Tapping the card or Reply navigates to HumanCoachChat (pass onChatClick lambda from App.kt)
Card is hidden (not shown at all) when lastCoachMessage is null
tasks.implementation_notes: Read HomeViewModel.kt and HomeState.kt carefully before modifying. Use the same async pattern as existing loadData() — add a chatDeferred alongside profileDeferred, workoutsDeferred, nutritionDeferred. The CoachMessagePreviewCard is a local composable inside HomeScreen.kt, not a separate file. Add onChatClick: () -> Unit parameter to HomeRoute and HomeScreen.

tasks.title: Add image upload support for chat messages
tasks.description: Implement image upload to Supabase Storage before sending image messages in chat.
acceptance_criteria: ChatStorageDataSource.uploadImage(userId, imageBytes): Result<String> uploads to Supabase Storage bucket 'chat-images' and returns the public URL
Filename is generated as '{userId}/{uuid}.jpg'
ChatRepositoryImpl.sendMessage() for MessageContent.Image uploads the image first via ChatStorageDataSource then stores the returned URL in the message
ChatViewModel handles SendImageMessage intent: sets isSending=true, dispatches sendMessage with the image bytes, clears selected image on success
ChatStorageDataSource is registered in chatModule as a single
Supabase Storage bucket 'chat-images' creation SQL is added to chat_messages.sql
tasks.implementation_notes: Check if supabase.storage is already a dependency in build.gradle.kts; if not, add libs.supabase.storage. Use supabase.storage.from('chat-images').upload(path, bytes). The existing MediaCapture infrastructure (MediaCapture.kt) provides the imageBytes from camera/gallery — in ChatInputBar, the onImageAttach callback should eventually connect to it.

tasks.title: Polish: error handling, offline states, AI streaming UX
tasks.description: Add robust error handling, offline detection, and smooth AI streaming text animation.
acceptance_criteria: Network error on message send shows a retry button on the failed message bubble
AI streaming timeout (>30s with no chunk) cancels the stream and shows an error state
Empty state is shown when messages list is empty (different copy for Human vs AI chat)
Snackbar for errors is dismissible and auto-hides after 4 seconds
AI streaming text renders with a blinking cursor indicator while isAiStreaming=true
ChatViewModel clears streamingText and resets isAiStreaming=false on stream completion or error
Pagination (LoadMoreMessages) triggers when user scrolls to the top of the LazyColumn
tasks.implementation_notes: For the retry button, add a retryMessageId: String? field to ChatState. For streaming cursor, use a simple animated alpha on a '|' character using animateFloatAsState with InfiniteRepeatableSpec. For scroll-to-load-more, use LazyListState.firstVisibleItemIndex == 0 detection in LaunchedEffect.
